description: A schematic diagram illustrating the signal processing workflow in THz time-domain spectroscopy (THz-TDS), where a time-domain electric field signal E(x0, y0, t) is transformed via a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) into its frequency-domain representation E(f) = A(f)exp[iφ(f)], separating amplitude and phase, and then a selected frequency component is inverse transformed (FFT⁻¹) back into a time-domain oscillation with specific amplitude A(x0, y0, f0) and phase φ(x0, y0, f0).
key_findings: THz-TDS captures both amplitude and phase information of the electric field by transforming time-domain data into the frequency domain.
Individual frequency components can be isolated and reconstructed back into time-domain signals with defined amplitude and phase.
suggested_knowledge_points: Relationship between time-domain and frequency-domain representations of signals
Role of FFT in spectroscopy data analysis
Physical meaning of amplitude and phase in electromagnetic waves
Reconstruction of signals using inverse Fourier transform
